retrieved_docs.title: Lensmaker’s Equation -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The lensmaker equation is a mathematical computation to determine the focal length of a lens in air. The focal length of a lens is defined as the distance from the center of the lens to an optical point to which all light rays are refracted. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Autorefractors -- Anatomy and Physiology -- Schiener's Principle
retrieved_docs.content: Schiener, in 1619 was the first to describe that the eyes refractive error can be determined by employing a double pinhole aperture before the pupil. He observed that when parallel rays of light are passed through the eye from a distant object, they are limited to two small bundles when a double pinhole is placed in front of the eye. In hypermetropic eyes, the bundle of rays is intersected by the retina before they join, and hence two small light spots are observed. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Schirmer Test -- Definition/Introduction -- Historical Notions
retrieved_docs.content: The test's name honors the German ophthalmologist who developed the technique in 1903. [22] The lacrimal glands produce tears, lubricating and shielding the eyes. Otto Schirmer used the test to examine the amount of tear production. In the original procedure, filter paper strips of size 5 mm by 35 mm were inserted into the eye's lower conjunctival sac, and the moistened area's length was measured after 5 minutes. The Schirmer test evaluated the quantity of tear production under typical circumstances.

retrieved_docs.title: Schirmer Test -- Definition/Introduction -- Test materials
retrieved_docs.content: The tools needed to perform the Schirmer test include the following (see Images. Schirmer Test Strip): Schirmer strips or filter paper (Whatman filter paper #41) with dimensions 35 mm and 5 mm [10] Topical anesthetic eye drops A timer or stopwatch Unmarked filter paper strips may be converted into Schirmer test strips by marking the sides at intervals of 5 mm with a ruler or measuring tape. The test duration is exactly 5 minutes.

retrieved_docs.title: Snellen Chart -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: ETDRS : Developed in 1982 by a researcher named Rick Ferris, the ETDRS is a modification of the Bailey-Lovie chart to overcome the limitations of the Snellen chart. The ETDRS chart is tested from a shorter distance (13 feet rather than 20 feet), has the same amount of letters in every row (five letters each), and has an equal spacing of both the letters and the rows on a logarithmic scale. Shamir et al. found the ETDRS to be significantly more accurate and reproducible than the Snellen chart, yet its adoption in clinical practice has been limited due to its cost, longer testing time, and size of the chart. [3] Jäger : Invented in 1854 by the Austrian ophthalmologist Jäger, this chart tests near vision. The card contains 11 short paragraphs, which are numbered from 1 (smallest font) to 11 (largest font). The card is held roughly 14 inches from the nose at a comfortable reading distance for testing.

retrieved_docs.title: Lensmaker’s Equation -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The lensmaker equation quantifies the relationship between the focal length, refractive index, thickness, and curvature of a lens and is used to produce a lens with a desired focal point; this is the ideal lens for a given refraction. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Schirmer Test -- Definition/Introduction -- Conducting the test
retrieved_docs.content: The Schirmer strips are gently removed once the 5-minute period is over. Afterward, the moistened section's length is measured from the baseline mark to the region's endpoint. Commercial Schirmer strips usually have markings for measurement in millimeters. Measurements should be taken separately for each eye.

retrieved_docs.title: Autorefractors -- Anatomy and Physiology -- Schiener's Principle
retrieved_docs.content: In myopic eyes, the two bundles of rays cross each other before falling on the retina, and thus two small spots of light are seen. The two points of light can be merged to form a single point by taking the double pinhole at the eye's far point. Hence, the ocular refractive error can be determined from the far point of the eye. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Empathy -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The term "empathy" (translated from the German word Einfuhlung , which means feeling into) started over 100 years ago and was introduced by psychologist Edward Titchener. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Schirmer Test -- Definition/Introduction -- Historical Notions
retrieved_docs.content: The Schirmer test has undergone modifications and enhancements since its conception, eg, adding a topical anesthetic to aid in differentiating between reflex tearing and baseline tear production. Compared to the non-anesthetized version, the anesthetized Schirmer test more accurately measures the eyes' intrinsic tear production capacity by numbing the ocular surface and inhibiting reflex tearing.

retrieved_docs.title: Amsler Sign -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Dr Marc Amsler (1891-1968) was a Swiss ophthalmologist and a great teacher. He was a student of Jules Gonin (1870-1935) at the University of Lausanne and a strong supporter of Gonin's ideas on the repair of rhegmatogenous retinal detachments. Dr Amsler was known as Le marcheur de l'operation de Gonin ("The seller of Gonin's surgical procedure"). [2]  He later became the chief of the Zurich Eye Clinic. His contributions to ophthalmology include the Amsler grid and the Amsler-Dubois chart. Amsler grid is a 10 cm × 10 cm square-shaped chart with 7 variants. The chart is a valuable and affordable tool for monitoring metamorphopsia and scotoma in various macular diseases, particularly in cases of wet age-related macular degeneration. [3] The Amsler-Dubois chart is essential for documenting posterior segment lesions, including retinal detachment.

retrieved_docs.title: Haidinger Brush Phenomenon -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The Haidinger brush phenomenon, also known as Haidinger brushes, is a distinctive and intriguing visual phenomenon that allows humans to perceive the polarization of light. This entoptic phenomenon is named after Austrian scientist Wilhelm Karl von Haidinger, who first reported the phenomenon in 1844. The Haidinger brush phenomenon is an elliptical, yellowish-blue pattern against a blue or white background when viewing polarized light. [1] The visualization of Haidinger brushes is a way of observing and potentially monitoring the density and condition of xanthophyll pigment in the macula and, in this manner identifying and tracking macular disease. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Schirmer Test -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The Schirmer test score is determined by measuring the length of the strips' moistened area against fluid movement duration in minutes. [2] A score greater than 15 mm in 5 minutes is considered normal. A score of less than 5 mm in 5 minutes indicates a tear deficiency. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Schwartze Sign -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The Schwartze sign is named after the German otologist Hermann Schwartze (1837-1910), who, in his day, was the first otologic professor in Germany. His work focussed on different otologic diseases, one of which being the ankylosis of the stapes now known as otosclerosis, a name defined several years later by Adam Politzer. Modern literature credit Hermann Schwartze with the discovery of a characteristic coloration of the promontory related to otosclerosis, but it remains unclear whether he used the term himself. [1] [2] Other terms used are Flemingo's flush sign and Rising sun sign. [3] The terms Schwartze and Schwartz are used interchangeably by different authors. To avoid any confusion, we will use the term Schwartze, named after the discoverer.

retrieved_docs.title: Lensmaker’s Equation -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The lensmaker equation may be used to design simple lenses, double-convex or double-concave, meniscus, and plano-convex or plano-concave lenses. The lensmaker equation also has utility in determining the optical power of intraocular lenses placed after cataract surgery. [3] [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Stark Law -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The term "referral" means a request for a specific or service by a physician for Medicare Part B services and/or a care plan that includes designated health services. Also, the term "financial relationship" includes investment interest, ownership, and compensation arrangements.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2908
retrieved_docs.content: Combined obstetrical version is a category of obstetric procedures, and internal and combined version without extraction is a specific type within this category, indicating that the latter is a subclass of the former. This relationship clarifies that internal and combined version without extraction falls under the broader concept of combined obstetrical version.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 2268
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy (الحمل) is a medical condition that is referenced by several synonymous or related terms in different languages, including “Zwangerschap als bijkomstige bevinding,” “těhotenství s paterčaty,” and “zwangerschap; zesling.” These terms all denote the state of gestation and are considered interchangeable descriptors of the same condition. A specific form of this condition, Septuplet pregnancy, results in the presence of multiple fetuses, illustrating that pregnancy can involve the development of several children simultaneously. Certain pharmacologic agents are contraindicated during pregnancy because of potential harm to the developing fetus; these include sodium iodide I123, norethindrone enanthate, testosterone, and tetracycline phosphate complex. The contraindication of norethindrone enanthate is explicitly linked to pregnancy as a disease state, reinforcing the broader principle that these drugs should not be administered to individuals who are pregnant.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 1 -- Community 216
retrieved_docs.content: or tissue transplantation). The unified taxonomy clarifies procedural indications, inter‑procedure connections, and associated pathologies, thereby supporting optimal patient assessment and surgical planning.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 2402
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy, referred to in Arabic as الحمل, is a central medical concept that is also described by the Dutch phrase “Zwangerschap als bijkomstige bevinding,” which is considered related and possibly synonymous with الحمل. Various forms of multiple gestation are recognized as specific subcategories of pregnancy: “Septuplet pregnancy” denotes a pregnancy with seven fetuses; the Czech term “těhotenství s paterčaty” and the Dutch expression “zwangerschap; zesling” both refer to pregnancies with multiple offspring; the Arabic phrases “حمل توأمي,” “حمل بثلاثة توائم,” and “حمل بأربعة توائم” describe twin, triplet, and quadruplet pregnancies respectively. These multiple‑gestation entities are linked to the general concept of الحمل through a “has child” relationship, indicating that they are particular types of pregnancy. Additionally, the medication tetracycline phosphate complex is identified as contraindicated in pregnancy, meaning that its use should be avoided in patients who are الحمل.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 5 -- Community 2954
retrieved_docs.content: The concept of an “Allowed qualifier” designates embryology as a relevant descriptor for thyroid disorders, retinal detachment (تمزق الشبكية), and lower‑extremity ulcers (قرحات الطرفين السفليين). Recognizing embryology as an appropriate qualifier highlights the importance of developmental origins in understanding and managing these diverse conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 4 -- Community 2865
retrieved_docs.content: Duration of gestation refers to the length of time a fetus remains in the uterus before birth, and the classification of a birth as liveborn is encompassed within this gestational timeframe; therefore, liveborn status is defined by the specific duration of gestation at delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 1 -- Community 161
retrieved_docs.content: scarring into a unified classification scheme that supports recognition, documentation, and management of pregnancy‑related complications across multiple languages and clinical decision‑making pathways.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 1 -- Community 174
retrieved_docs.content: The period of 29‑30 weeks of gestation completed is linked to the outcome of a liveborn infant, indicating that reaching this gestational age can result in a live birth. Liveborn is classified within the broader concept of duration of gestation, reflecting its role as a specific gestational outcome. In the context of health‑care utilization, individuals who encounter health services for reproductive reasons (coded Z30‑Z39) are organized under the liveborn category, emphasizing that these service encounters often pertain to the care of newborns. The specific service “Controle van zwangerschap met verhoogd risico, niet gespecificeerd” (control of pregnancy with increased risk, not specified) is a child concept of the Z30‑Z39 reproductive health services category, illustrating a more detailed level of care for high‑risk pregnancies. The overarching classification “PERSONS ENCOUNTERING HEALTH SERVICES IN CIRCUMSTANCES RELATED TO REPRODUCTION AND DEVELOPMENT” has a narrower relationship to the Z30‑Z39 group, situating the latter as a sub‑category within the broader reproductive and developmental health services framework. Additionally, the procedure “Observation and evaluation of newborns for suspected condition not found” is a type of medical examination/evaluation and is also placed under the broader reproductive health services category, highlighting that newborn assessment for absent suspected conditions is both a clinical evaluation and part of the spectrum of reproductive health encounters. Together, these relationships delineate how gestational timing, livebirth outcomes, reproductive health service categories, high‑risk pregnancy management, and newborn evaluation are interconnected within medical classification systems and patient‑care pathways.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 1 -- Community 65
retrieved_docs.content: tapping (بضع الصدر), linking thoracic interventions, while the unspecified after‑care concept “Nabehandeling met plastische chirurgie, niet gespecificeerd” is mapped as “other aftercare” without a defined target. Collectively, these relationships delineate a structured taxonomy of female‑organ diagnostic and therapeutic procedures, clarify coding exclusions for unrelated interventions, and emphasize the central responsibility of Obstetrics & Gynecology specialists in performing and overseeing these services across multiple languages and classification systems.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 3 -- Community 2024
retrieved_docs.content: Zwangerschap als bijkomstige bevinding and الحمل are considered synonymous terms for pregnancy across Dutch and Arabic language contexts. Testosterone and tetracycline phosphate complex are identified as drugs that are contraindicated during الحمل, highlighting the need to avoid these medications in any pregnant patient. Various forms of multiple gestation are linked to الحمل as specific subtypes of pregnancy: حمل توأمي denotes a twin pregnancy, حمل بثلاثة توائم denotes a triplet pregnancy, حمل بأربعة توائم denotes a quadruplet pregnancy, and Septuplet pregnancy represents a pregnancy with seven fetuses. The concept Septuplet pregnancy is further connected to الحمل through a “has child” relationship, indicating that it is a particular instance of pregnancy. Similar “has child” connections associate الحمل with the Czech term těhotenství s paterčaty and the Dutch phrase zwangerschap; zesling, both of which also refer to multiple‑gestation pregnancies. Together, these relationships illustrate the multilingual terminology for pregnancy and its variants, and underscore the clinical importance of recognizing contraindicated drugs such as testosterone and tetracycline phosphate complex in all forms of pregnancy, especially in high‑order multiple gestations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2320
retrieved_docs.content: The gestational period of 29‑30 weeks completed is directly linked to the outcome of a liveborn infant, indicating that reaching this stage of pregnancy can result in a newborn who is alive at birth. In the broader classification of pregnancy timelines, the concept of Duration of gestation encompasses liveborn as a specific category or outcome, reflecting that livebirth is considered one of the possible results within the overall spectrum of gestational durations. This relationship underscores the clinical relevance of gestational age in determining the likelihood of a live birth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 1 -- Community 131
retrieved_docs.content: maternal conditions that influence obstetric management. * The **maternal pregnancy period** also encompasses routine events such as **normal delivery** and other indications for care in pregnancy, labor, and delivery. These are grouped under “other pregnancy and delivery including normal,” which is linked to the Czech concept **výsledek těhotenství** (pregnancy outcome) and to specific delivery codes such as “Spontane enkelvoudige bevalling, niet gespecificeerd” (spontaneous single delivery, unspecified). Findings related to pregnancy are inversely related to pregnancy outcome, indicating that each clinical observation contributes to the overall assessment of delivery success. Collectively, the hierarchy demonstrates how broad pregnancy‑related complication categories are subdivided into precise obstetric entities—cervical abnormalities, hemorrhagic disorders, hypertensive and metabolic conditions, uterine growth discrepancies, fetal presentation issues, and outcomes of pregnancy

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 4 -- Community 2733
retrieved_docs.content: Internal and combined version without extraction is a specific form of internal obstetrical version; it represents an obstetric maneuver in which the fetus is turned internally without extraction and is classified under the broader category of internal obstetrical versions.

Multiple‑gestation pregnancies are enumerated as twin pregnancy (حمل توأمي), triplet pregnancy (حمل بثلاثة توائم), quadruplet pregnancy (حمل بأربعة توائم), sextuplet pregnancy (zwangerschap; zesling), septuplet pregnancy, and the generic “pregnancy with multiple fetuses” (těhotenství s paterčaty). Each is linked to the general concept of pregnancy (الحمل), with “Zwangerschap als bijkomstige bevinding” noted as a synonym. Pharmacologic contraindications in pregnancy are highlighted by listing isotretinoin, tretinoin, tetracycline phosphate complex, and testosterone under “contraindicated_with_disease” for pregnancy, emphasizing the need for careful medication review in expectant mothers. Isotretinoin and tretinoin are also recognized as topical retinoids used in dermatology for acne. Placental pathology is represented by intervillous hemorrhage of the placenta, linked to the child

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 2604
retrieved_docs.content: Spontane enkelvoudige bevalling, niet gespecificeerd is identified as a specific term that maps to the broader category “other pregnancy and delivery including normal,” indicating that this designation refers to a spontaneous single delivery that is not further specified and is encompassed within the general classification of various pregnancy and delivery events, including normal births.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1787
retrieved_docs.content: The clinical entity c.bevalling; misvorming, bekken (benig) denotes a pelvic malformation that can cause a difficult labor, as illustrated by the phrase Moeilijke bevalling door bekkenmisvorming, which is mapped to this concept. This condition is identified as an indication for specialized care or intervention related to labor and delivery, underscoring the importance of targeted obstetric management when pelvic malformation is present.
